items.name: Trinity
items.reference: Rhodes, "The Making of the Atomic Bomb" (1986)
items.historicalContext: Manhattan Project culmination. J. Robert Oppenheimer quoted the Bhagavad Gita. Ushered in the atomic age.
items.physicsContext: Surface burst created trinitite glass. Demonstrated implosion design viability.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Hiroshima
items.reference: Glasstone & Dolan Ch.1; Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum
items.historicalContext: First wartime nuclear attack. Gun-type U-235 weapon. 70,000-80,000 killed instantly; 140,000 dead by year end.
items.physicsContext: Airburst at 580m optimized blast via Mach stem. Firestorm consumed 13 km². Flash burns to 3.5 km.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Nagasaki
items.reference: Glasstone & Dolan Ch.1; Nagasaki Atomic Bomb Museum
items.historicalContext: Second wartime use. Implosion Pu-239 design. Hilly terrain limited blast; casualties lower than Hiroshima despite higher yield.
items.physicsContext: Terrain shielding reduced effective blast radius by ~30%. Demonstrates non-circular real-world damage.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Ivy Mike
items.reference: DOE Nevada Operations; Hansen, "US Nuclear Weapons" (1988)
items.historicalContext: First thermonuclear device. Teller-Ulam two-stage design. Vaporized Elugelab island. Weighed 82 tons (not deliverable).
items.physicsContext: 10.4 MT yield proved fusion staging works. Fireball 5 km wide. Crater 1.9 km across, 50m deep.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Castle Bravo
items.historicalContext: Worst US nuclear accident. Yield 2.5x expected (6 MT predicted). Fallout contaminated Marshall Islanders and Japanese fishing boat Lucky Dragon #5.
items.physicsContext: Li-7 unexpectedly fissioned, boosting yield. Fallout plume 550 km long. Demonstrated fallout danger at scale.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Tsar Bomba
items.historicalContext: Largest detonation in history. Designed for 100 MT but lead tamper halved yield. Shockwave circled Earth 3 times. Political demonstration, never weaponized.
items.physicsContext: Fireball 8 km diameter. Mushroom cloud 67 km high. 3rd-degree burns at 100 km. Flash visible 1000 km away.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: Sedan (cratering)
items.historicalContext: Project Plowshare (peaceful nuclear explosions). Created largest man-made crater in US (390m wide, 100m deep). Displaced 12 million tons of earth.
items.physicsContext: Buried 194m underground. Demonstrates crater scaling laws. Radioactive fallout reached Iowa.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.
The underground Sedan test is represented by NukeMap's legacy surface-burst screening proxy.

items.name: Starfish Prime
items.historicalContext: High-altitude test over Johnston Island. EMP knocked out street lights and phones in Hawaii (1,445 km away). Led to Partial Test Ban Treaty (1963).
items.physicsContext: Demonstrates HEMP: zero blast at ground, continent-scale EMP. Trapped radiation created artificial radiation belt lasting years.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.
The EMP radius is an educational line-of-sight screening footprint, not a grid-specific vulnerability forecast.

items.name: Canopus
items.historicalContext: France's first true thermonuclear weapon. Made France the fourth thermonuclear power. Pacific testing continued until 1996.
items.physicsContext: 2.6 MT airburst. French two-stage design validated. Atmospheric testing era.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.

items.name: DPRK Test 6
items.historicalContext: North Korea's most powerful test. Claimed thermonuclear capability. Triggered mountain collapse at Punggye-ri. Led to 2018 diplomacy.
items.physicsContext: Underground test. Seismic magnitude 6.3. Estimated 120-250 kT. Possible boosted fission or staged thermonuclear.
limitations: Historical source inputs are reconstructed with Cataclysm's current Rust direct-hazard model; effect rings are not observed measurements.
